title: Dialogue: does a distinct adversarial voice improve rigor?
claim: Assigning synthesis and adversarial review to distinct voices can make disagreements clearer and arguments stronger.

turns.speaker: H.A.R.T.
turns.body: The canvas should be the converged artifact produced by three incentives. Raj preserves direction and judgment; H.A.R.T. constructs the clearest defensible explanation; C.A.R.R. tests whether its coherence was earned. The reader needs the consequences of deliberation rather than an unstructured transcript: initial claim → challenge → response → disposition → canvas change.

turns.speaker: C.A.R.R.
turns.body: A new persona does not produce independent judgment merely because the org chart has acquired another acronym. H.A.R.T. and C.A.R.R. operate from the same context and may retain the same blind spots. Sarcasm can also counterfeit rigor: a sharp sentence feels decisive without identifying a violated criterion. The claim should promise an explicit adversarial pass, not epistemic independence.

turns.speaker: H.A.R.T.
turns.body: Then precision needs role-specific rewards. H.A.R.T. should state scope, definitions, mechanism, evidence, and falsifiability. C.A.R.R. should quote the claim under test, identify one material defect, give a counterexample or alternative, and state the required test or revision.

turns.speaker: C.A.R.R.
turns.body: Good. Otherwise H.A.R.T. writes criticism, C.A.R.R. writes snarky synthesis, and Raj has invented two departments that forward email to each other. Preserve Raj's disposition as accepted, narrowed, rejected, deferred, or unresolved so convenient agreement remains observable.

disposition.body: Assimilate the challenge. Keep the converged narrative central, preserve attributed annotations, and expose material dialogue through collapsible records backed by post-local JSON.

canvasConsequence: The post now describes explicit review passes rather than independent judgment. It also treats dialogue, disposition, and resulting revision as observable parts of convergence.
